stimulus.content:
A table shows population density for four regions:

• Region A: 5 people per square kilometer (desert)
• Region B: 350 people per square kilometer (river delta)
• Region C: 2 people per square kilometer (arctic tundra)
• Region D: 90 people per square kilometer (grassland)

stem: Which region most likely has the highest population density, and why?

choices.text: Region B, because river deltas provide rich farmland
Region A, because deserts have plenty of open space
Region C, because cold areas attract many people
Region D, because grasslands have no other uses

answer_explanation: Region B has the highest density at 350 people per square kilometer because river deltas provide fertile soil, fresh water, and flat land ideal for farming and settlement.
